findings.one_line: From `lem:clip-bias`, the declared `L2` nuisance assumptions reproduce either an additive first-order drift `r_{mu,n} r_{e,n}/q + r_{mu,n} q^{1/(2 gamma)}` or, using only bounded nuisances, a localized drift `((r_{e,n}/q) + q^{1/(2 gamma)}) R_P(pi)^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)}`; the proof's smaller localized product term `((r_{mu,n} r_{e,n}/q) + r_{mu,n} q^{1/(2 gamma)}) R_P(pi)^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)}` is not derivable from the declared dependencies.

Re-deriving the localized empirical-process radius for `(pi-pi_star) Gamma_q(O; etahat)` from `def:clipped-aipw-score`, `ass:bounded-outcome`, and `ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances` gives at best `||(pi-pi_star) Gamma_q||_{L2(P)} \lesssim q^{-1} P_X(D_pi)^{1/2}`, not the proof's `q^{-1/2} P_X(D_pi)^{1/2}`; display (4) drops a factor `q^{-1/2}`, so the optimized exponent `zeta(a,c,gamma)` is not discharged.

The headline two-sided bracket and phase split are read off `oeq:feasible-upper`; once the unreproduced drift and variance terms are restored, the claimed upper rate `M_n <= C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p` and the equal-exponent/gap dichotomy are not established on the present DAG.

The membership proof explicitly uses i.i.d. sampling, consistency, unconfoundedness, and positivity to construct the witness law and verify class membership, but `ass:iid`, `ass:consistency`, `ass:unconfoundedness`, and `ass:positivity` are absent from `depends_on`; the node is not reproducible from its declared dependencies alone.
